insights.topic: Receipt vs. probabilistic signal cost delta
insights.insight: Ad tech charges $5–$150 CPA for modeled attribution; this protocol settles at roughly $0.10 per cryptographically verified purchase event — a 50–200x cost difference that is structural, not incremental.

With Chrome completing cookie deprecation and device fingerprinting under regulatory pressure, the ad stack has no remaining cross-site deterministic signal — verified receipt data fills that gap without identity attachment.

insights.topic: The inbox as the only cross-platform purchase record
insights.insight: Amazon sees Amazon, Walmart sees Walmart — no platform sees the full consumer purchase picture except the email inbox, which receives receipts from every vendor; this protocol is the first to make that record queryable without exposing identity.

4,001 verified receipts representing $233K in GMV took months to accumulate; any competitor starts at zero, and every new receipt compounds the signal density — the data corpus, not the underlying DKIM technology, is the defensible asset.

insights.topic: SaaS and cloud spend entering the dataset
insights.insight: Five pending vendor requests cover developer infrastructure (Vercel, Render, DigitalOcean, Hetzner) — if approved, this marks the first expansion beyond physical retail into digital subscription spend, broadening the signal for B2B and SaaS-adjacent marketers.

27 CBSAs active with recent expansion into rural Mountain West suggests social-graph propagation rather than paid acquisition; the dataset is trending toward national household spend representation, not a metro-skewed panel.
